content.full_text:
State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission

P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance Administration • • • Executive Legal • • • Enforcement • • • Conflict of Interest

(916) 322-5660                322-5901              327-6441              322-6444

November 10, 1977

                                                                    77-11-076

Mr. John C. Gee
Post Office Box 963
Boulder Creek, California 95006

Dear Mr. Gee:

     Thank you for your letter of September 30, 1977,
regarding the disqualification provisions of the Political Reform Act. I hope that the following advice
issued pursuant to Government Code §83114(b) and 2
Cal. Adm. Code §18329 will be helpful.

     The facts, as we understand them, are as follows:
Your wife is a party in litigation which is currently
pending in the San Lorenzo Valley County Water District
regarding the logging of watershed. Neither you nor
your wife has any monetary interest in the outcome of
the lawsuit. You do not depend on the creek that runs
through the proposed logging area, nor do you draw
your water from this source. You do, however, own
a well which we assume is used in connection with
your personal residence. With these facts as background, you have asked whether you must disqualify
yourself from matters pertaining to the watershed
should you be elected to the San Lorenzo Valley
County Water District Board of Directors.

     The Political Reform Act sets forth the basic
disqualification standard in §87100:

          No public official at any level of state
          or local government shall make, participate
          in making or in any way attempt to use his
          official position to influence a governmental decision in which he knows or has
          reason to know he has a financial interest.

John C. Gee
November 10, 1977
Page Two


     The Act provides, in §87103, that an official has a
financial interest in a governmental decision if it is
reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a
material financial effect, distinguishable from the
effect on the public generally, on a source of income,
an investment or an interest in real property.  Assuming
that your statement that neither you nor your wife has
any monetary interest in the outcome of the lawsuit
means that no sources of income, investments or interests
in real property will be foreseeably and materially
affected by any Board decisions relative to the logging
of watershed, you need not disqualify yourself from
participating in such Board actions.

     If you have any further questions, please feel free
to contact either me or Dwight Dickerson.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [illegible]
                                        Ted Prim, Chief
                                        Conflicts of Interest
                                        Division


TP:gb

P.O. Box 963
Boulder Creek, CA 95006
September 30, 1977

Political Fair Practices Commission
P.O. Box 307
Sacramento, CA 95804

Gentlemen,

I would like a written reply regarding my eligibility to vote on matters pertaining to the logging of watershed land should I be elected to the San Lorenzo Valley County Water District Board of Directors, on November 8th.

I am enclosing a copy of the lawsuit currently pending on this matter. My wife is a party in this lawsuit. The basic question addressed by the suit is whether or not the water district may log this land without first obtaining an environmental impact report. We do not have any monetary interest in the outcome of this lawsuit, and we do not depend on the river (that flows through the proposed logging area) for our water supply. We have a well. Our interest in this suit is one of principle, that the proposed logging is environmentally unsound.

Additionally, I have made it very clear in my campaign that I oppose logging this land.

I would appreciate as prompt a reply as possible as this issue will undoubtedly be raised during the campaign.

Sincerely,

John C. Dee
John C. Dee
